title: Does particle wave matter really exist
display_title: Does particle wave matter really exist
wikitext:
[]: Does particle-wave matter really exist?             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Mon Jan 18, 2010 4:41 pm I have doubts that PWM really exists the way everyone seems to be throwing it out. For one thing, it is completely fan-created and appears nowhere in official Evangelion materials. "Normal" matter exhibits wave and particle duality, so the term doesn't make much internal sense. The quote that the term derives from seems to me more like a technobabble explanation that the angels are "scifi energy beings." At several points supposed PWM is seen collapsing (often violently) upon destruction of an angel's core, and even Rei III begins to decompose when her abnormally powerful ATF becomes unstable (although this is due to Destrudo rather than the field actually weakening in any way). In fact, I would go so far as to state that Angels are the only creatures that need their ATF in order to remain physically stable (since their physical forms are determined by the field itself), while LDO, being genetically determined in form, only transform into LCL due to Destrudo. Destrudo reactions also have only been known to occur under special circumstances (such as Contact Experiments and Anti-ATF), and no LDO is seen capable of becoming LCL on its own (hence why the Rei clones can exist without souls, because an ATF is not needed to maintain the body). In this case PWM, rather than a substance that exists outside of an ATF, is the substance created by an Angel's ATF when it rejects its genetically determined humanoid form. PWM itself is a manifestation of the Angels' unique ATF, and cannot exist without it. Hence, they "transcend concepts of living and non-living" as stated in the CI. The EVAs are what Angels would look like if they could not alter their forms. Adam and Lilith, rather than being made of PWM, may simply be different forms of "life," since they both predate LDO and ADO and have abnormally powerful ATF. Rei, Kaworu, and Rei's clones would then be composed of normal human bodies. Although this does beg the question of why some angels leave behind corpses and bleed and still have human DNA despite being "energy beings." Although it could be explained by PWM having both "normal" matter and ATF components, and reverting to "normal" matter in the lack of a suitable ATF. The often violent self-destructing may be a function of the collapsing ATF/S2 releasing stored energy. What precise difference exists between PWM and normal matter will be forever unknown. EDIT: This was just one big brainfart. There are so many holes in this that I'm surprised I came up with it.

[]: Re: Does particle-wave matter really exist?             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Jan 18, 2010 4:54 pm Zenoseiya wrote: Adam and Lilith, rather than being made of PWM, may simply be different forms of "life," since they both predate LDO and ADO and have abnormally powerful ATF. Rei, Kaworu, and Rei's clones would then be composed of normal human bodies.

[]: Re: Does particle-wave matter really exist?             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Mon Jan 18, 2010 5:12 pm I totally forgot about that! Probably has something to do with the Seeds themselves, I'd think. Abnormally powerful ATF and predating other forms of life and all. EDIT: In all fairness, the "science" of NGE is regular old nonsensical Technobabble. I doubt even Anno had all these theories in mind when he was making the series, and simply threw in what sounded good at the time.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jan 18, 2010 5:49 pm Episode #05 wrote: Misato (OFF): What the heck is this? RITSUKO: That's the code number for "cannot be analyzed". Misato: You mean, you don't know what the heck it is? RITSUKO: Correct. We do know the Angel is composed of a type of matter characterized by both particulate and wave properties, like light .

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jan 18, 2010 5:50 pm PWM is one of the bits where the Gainax rubber science goes "Ping!" since normal matter has been shown to act as both particle and wave in molecular size chunks. It could be a throwback to the alternative periodic tables that appeared in long-ago SF (like the Lux and Relux materials in John W. Campbell's Arcot, Wade and Morey super-science sequence) or the more modern counterparts which play mix and match with quarks and/or supersymmetric particles.

Article: Does particle wave matter really exist
 rubber science goes "Ping!" since normal matter has been shown to act as both particle and wave in molecular size chunks. It could be a throwback to the alternative periodic tables that appeared in long-ago SF (like the Lux and Relux materials in John W. Campbell's Arcot, Wade and Morey super-science sequence) or the more modern counterparts which play mix and match with quarks and/or supersymmetric particles.
